must_have.title: Digital Order Management
must_have.description: Waiters create and manage customer orders digitally via tablet/smartphone. Orders are sent directly to the kitchen display in real-time.

user_story.action: input customer orders on a tablet and send them to the kitchen instantly
user_story.benefit: eliminate handwritten miscommunication and create a digital record of every order

fields.name: table_number
fields.note: Selected from table list or manual input

fields.name: menu_items
fields.note: Array of selected menu items with quantity

fields.name: order_status
fields.note: pending | cooking | ready | served | paid | cancelled | voided

fields.name: is_additional
fields.note: Boolean — true if this is an additional order to existing table order

business_rules: Order must have at least one menu item
Order status updates must be real-time across all devices
Each order is linked to a specific table
If a table already has an active (unpaid) order, cashier can ADD items to that existing order instead of creating a new one
Additional items are appended to the same order with 'Tambahan' label
Additional items are sent to kitchen as new items with same order ID
Order total recalculated automatically after adding items
Order can be cancelled before payment (status → cancelled)
acceptance_criteria: Waiter can create a new order by selecting menu items and assigning to a table
If table already has active order, waiter sees option to 'Tambah Pesanan' instead of creating new order
Additional items are appended to existing order and sent to kitchen
Order total updates automatically after adding items
Order appears on Kitchen Display within 2 seconds of submission
Order total is auto-calculated based on menu item prices and quantities
Special notes are visible to the chef on Kitchen Display

must_have.title: Kitchen Display System
must_have.description: Real-time kitchen display with detailed order→cook→ready→serve flow. Chef marks items as cooking/ready, waiter gets notification when food is ready to serve. Clear status tracking between kitchen and cashier.

user_story.action: view incoming orders on a dedicated kitchen screen, mark them as cooking or ready, and notify waiter
user_story.benefit: receive orders clearly without verbal miscommunication and ensure timely food delivery

fields.name: elapsed_time
fields.note: Live timer showing how long since order placed

business_rules: New orders appear at the top of the queue with audio/visual alert
Chef taps to change status: pending → cooking → ready
When chef marks 'ready': notification sent to waiter/cashier POS screen immediately
Waiter sees notification badge with table number and 'Pesanan Siap!' message
Waiter taps notification to confirm 'Sudah Diantar' → status changes to 'served'
Elapsed time shown on each order card (how long since ordered)
Orders exceeding target time (e.g., 15 min) are highlighted in red as late
Additional items (tambahan) from same table appear as new items in the SAME order card
Kitchen display auto-removes served orders after confirmation
acceptance_criteria: Kitchen display shows all pending and cooking orders in queue format
Chef can update order status with a single tap
Recipe/cooking guide is accessible from each order item
When order is marked 'ready', waiter gets real-time notification on POS screen
Notification shows table number and items that are ready
Waiter can mark order as 'served' from notification
Elapsed time counter visible on each order
Late orders (>15 min) visually highlighted
Completed orders move to done section

must_have.title: Cashier & Payment Processing
must_have.description: Automatic bill calculation with PPN tax, payment processing, success popup with print receipt, and receipt reprint from transaction history.

user_story.action: generate an accurate bill with PPN for a table and process payment with receipt
user_story.benefit: eliminate manual calculation errors, comply with tax requirements, and provide professional receipts

fields.name: payment_method
fields.note: tunai | qris | gopay | ovo | dana | shopeepay | transfer_bank — stores SPECIFIC method, not generic category

fields.name: digital_payment_ref
fields.note: Reference number for digital payments (QRIS transaction ID, e-wallet ref, transfer ref)

fields.name: transfer_proof_photo
fields.note: Photo/image of transfer proof uploaded by cashier (for transfer_bank payments)

fields.name: amount_paid
fields.note: Amount given by customer (for cash) or confirmed amount (for digital)

fields.name: change
fields.note: Auto-calculated for cash. Always 0 for digital payments.

business_rules: Bill shows subtotal, discount (if any), PPN amount, and grand total separately
PPN rate is pulled from system settings (F-016)
Discount applied before PPN: (Subtotal - Discount) × PPN rate
Payment cannot be processed if amount paid is less than grand total
CASH: Numpad input for amount received, auto-calculate change
QRIS: Show static QR code image (uploaded by owner in settings). Cashier confirms payment received manually.

E-WALLET (GoPay/OVO/Dana/ShopeePay): Cashier selects provider, system shows QR code for that specific provider (uploaded by owner in settings). Cashier confirms payment received, optionally inputs reference number
TRANSFER BANK: Cashier confirms transfer received, optionally inputs reference number, can upload photo of transfer proof (bukti transfer)
For digital payments: no change calculation needed (exact amount)
Transaction stores SPECIFIC payment method name (e.g., 'GoPay', 'OVO', 'Transfer BCA') — NOT generic 'Digital'
Success popup displays the specific payment method used (e.g., 'GoPay', not 'Digital')
Transaction history shows specific method name in table column
After successful payment, a success popup appears with transaction summary
Success popup includes Print Struk button and Selesai button
Receipt includes itemized list, subtotal, PPN breakdown, and grand total
Transaction is recorded in sales history with full PPN info
Any past transaction receipt can be reprinted from Transaction History (S-012)
acceptance_criteria: Payment screen shows subtotal, PPN %, PPN amount, and grand total
Cashier can select payment method and input amount received
Change is calculated against grand total (inclusive PPN)
After payment: success popup with checkmark, summary, and Print Struk button
Cashier can print receipt from success popup
Cashier can tap Selesai to return to order list
Past transactions can be viewed in detail and receipt reprinted from S-012
Completed payment updates order status to paid

must_have.title: Inventory / Stock Management
must_have.description: Track all ingredient and supply stock levels. Stock automatically decreases when orders are placed based on recipe ingredient requirements.

user_story.action: monitor real-time stock levels and see automatic deductions when orders are processed
user_story.benefit: prevent inventory loss, know exactly what is in stock, and identify discrepancies

business_rules: Stock deduction is triggered automatically when an order is confirmed
Deduction amounts are based on recipe ingredient definitions
Stock cannot go below zero — system warns if insufficient
Only authorized users can manually adjust stock (restock or correction)
acceptance_criteria: Owner can view all inventory items with current stock levels
Stock auto-decreases when orders are placed based on recipe ingredients
Owner can manually add stock (restocking)
System shows stock movement history (in/out log)

must_have.title: Menu Management
must_have.description: Create, edit, and organize menu items with pricing, categories, and availability status.

user_story.action: manage all menu items including prices, categories, and availability from one place
user_story.benefit: keep menu up-to-date and control what is available for ordering

business_rules: Unavailable items are hidden from the order screen
Price changes take effect immediately for new orders
Each menu item can be linked to a recipe and ingredient list
acceptance_criteria: Owner can add, edit, and delete menu items
Menu items can be categorized and filtered
Availability toggle immediately affects order screen
Menu items can be linked to recipe guides and inventory ingredients

must_have.title: Recipe Guide / Cooking Instructions
must_have.description: Each menu item can have a detailed cooking guide with step-by-step instructions, required ingredients with measurements, and cooking time. Accessible from Kitchen Display.

user_story.action: view the cooking guide for any menu item directly from the kitchen display while preparing orders
user_story.benefit: ensure consistent food quality and enable any chef to prepare any dish correctly

fields.name: ingredients
fields.note: Array of ingredients with quantities and units

business_rules: Recipe ingredients must reference inventory items for stock deduction
Only Owner or authorized chef can edit recipes
Recipe is viewable from Kitchen Display per order item
acceptance_criteria: Owner can create and edit recipe guides for each menu item
Recipe shows ingredients, step-by-step instructions, and estimated cooking time
Chef can open recipe guide from Kitchen Display with one tap on a menu item
Recipe ingredients are linked to inventory items

must_have.title: Daily Sales Report & Dashboard
must_have.description: Automated daily sales summary showing total revenue, number of orders, top-selling items, and sales trends. Accessible as a dashboard for the owner.

user_story.action: view a daily sales dashboard showing revenue, order count, and best-selling items
user_story.benefit: monitor business performance in real-time and make informed decisions

fields.name: date_range
fields.note: Today, this week, this month, custom range

business_rules: Report data updates in real-time as transactions are completed
Only completed (paid) orders are counted in revenue
Dashboard is accessible from any device
acceptance_criteria: Owner can view todays sales summary on a dashboard
Dashboard shows total revenue, order count, and top-selling items
Owner can filter reports by date range
Data refreshes automatically without manual action

must_have.title: Checklist-based Access Control
must_have.description: Owner can configure permissions for each user/role via a checklist UI. Each module access can be toggled on/off per user.

user_story.action: configure exactly which modules each staff member can access using a simple checklist interface
user_story.benefit: have granular control over who sees what, adapting to multi-role staff flexibility

business_rules: Only Owner can access the access control panel
Permission changes take effect immediately
Each role has default permissions that can be overridden per individual user
Core permissions cannot be removed
acceptance_criteria: Owner can view a list of all users with their current permissions
Owner can toggle each permission on/off via checkboxes
Changes apply immediately without requiring user to re-login
Default permissions are pre-set based on role but fully customizable

must_have.title: Multi-role Authentication / Login
must_have.description: Secure login system with role-based routing. Each user logs in and is directed to their appropriate interface based on their role and permissions.

user_story.action: create user accounts with assigned roles so each staff member has their own login
user_story.benefit: ensure accountability, track who did what, and enforce access control

business_rules: Each user must have a unique username
After login, ALL users are routed to the Main Dashboard Hub (S-016)
Dashboard Hub shows role-appropriate menu options
Owner can create, edit, and deactivate user accounts
Failed login attempts are logged
Owner can set and reset PIN per user for shift operations
acceptance_criteria: Users can log in with username and password
All users are routed to Main Dashboard Hub after login
Dashboard Hub shows role-appropriate menu cards
Owner can manage all user accounts (create, edit, deactivate, set PIN)
Logged-in users name is displayed in the interface

must_have.title: Cash Register Shift Management (Open/Close Kasir)
must_have.description: PIC cashier must verify PIN, then enter the opening cash balance before processing any payments. At closing, cashier enters a one-way closing session: can print detailed transaction report line-by-line, counts physical cash, and system compares with expected amount. If discrepancy exists, notes are MANDATORY before closing.

user_story.action: open a shift with opening cash balance and close it by reconciling physical cash with system records
user_story.benefit: ensure full accountability for cash handling and detect discrepancies immediately

fields.name: pin_verified
fields.note: Boolean — cashier must verify 4-6 digit PIN before opening shift

fields.name: opening_balance
fields.note: Cash amount entered at shift start (modal awal operasional)

fields.name: actual_cash
fields.note: Physical cash counted by cashier at closing

fields.name: discrepancy
fields.note: Difference between expected and actual cash (surplus/shortage)

fields.name: total_revenue
fields.note: Total all payments (cash + digital) during shift

fields.name: closing_notes
fields.note: Optional notes from cashier explaining discrepancy

business_rules: Cashier MUST verify PIN before opening a shift
Cashier MUST re-verify PIN every time they enter POS operational screens (S-002, S-003, S-004, S-005) from outside POS — this prevents transaction manipulation during active shift
PIN is NOT required when navigating WITHIN POS screens (e.g., S-002 to S-003 to S-004)
PIN is required when entering POS FROM: Dashboard Hub, admin screens (stock, menu), or any non-POS screen
Cashier MUST open a shift before processing any payment
Only one active shift per cashier at a time
Opening balance is the starting cash in the register (modal awal operasional)
Cashier can do pre-shift preparation (check menu, stock) before opening shift
During shift, all cash and digital transactions are tracked
At closing, system calculates expected cash = opening + cash_in - cash_out
Closing is a ONE-WAY session — cashier cannot return to operations after initiating close
Cashier can print detailed line-by-line transaction report during closing for physical cash tracing
Cashier inputs actual physical cash count
System shows discrepancy (surplus or shortage) with clear indicator
If discrepancy exists (not match), notes are MANDATORY — cashier must explain before closing
If cash matches (green), notes are optional
Closed shift data is immutable and logged for audit
Owner can view all shift history and discrepancy reports
acceptance_criteria: Cashier sees Open Shift option on Main Dashboard Hub
Cashier must enter correct PIN before accessing shift opening
Cashier enters opening cash balance and confirms to start shift
During active shift, current shift info is visible in header
Entering POS screens from outside POS always requires PIN re-verification
Navigating within POS screens does not require PIN
Cashier can initiate Close Shift — this enters a one-way closing session
In closing session, cashier CANNOT navigate back to operations
Close Shift screen shows full shift summary and line-by-line transaction detail
Cashier can print the transaction report for physical tracing
System calculates and displays discrepancy with color indicator
If discrepancy exists, notes field is mandatory and close button is disabled until filled
If cash matches, closing completes directly
Owner can view shift history with all opening/closing data and discrepancies
Non-owner users see quick-access bar on admin screens to return to Hub or their primary workspace

must_have.title: POS System Settings (Tax/PPN Configuration)
must_have.description: System-wide settings for the POS including configurable PPN/tax rate applied to all transactions. Owner can enable/disable PPN and set the percentage. PPN is automatically calculated on every transaction.

user_story.action: configure PPN/tax rate and other POS system settings
user_story.benefit: ensure all transactions comply with tax regulations and settings can be adjusted as tax rates change

fields.name: ppn_enabled
fields.note: Boolean — whether PPN is applied to transactions

fields.name: ppn_rate
fields.note: Percentage (e.g., 11 for 11% PPN). Applied to subtotal.

fields.name: receipt_footer
fields.note: Custom footer text for receipts (e.g., 'Terima kasih!')

business_rules: PPN rate is configurable by owner in system settings
PPN is calculated as: ppn_amount = subtotal * (ppn_rate / 100)
Grand total = subtotal + ppn_amount
PPN applies to all transactions when enabled
PPN breakdown must be visible on payment screen and on printed receipt
Changes to PPN rate apply to new transactions only, not retroactively
acceptance_criteria: Owner can access POS Settings from admin panel or Dashboard Hub
Owner can toggle PPN on/off
Owner can set PPN rate percentage
Payment screen shows subtotal, PPN amount, and grand total
Printed receipt includes PPN breakdown
Transaction history shows PPN info per transaction

must_have.title: Void / Cancel Order & Transaction
must_have.description: Mechanism to void or cancel an order before or after payment. Voided items are tracked for audit and affect shift reconciliation correctly. PIN required for void.

user_story.action: void a wrong order or cancel a transaction when customer changes mind
user_story.benefit: correct mistakes without affecting cash reconciliation accuracy

fields.name: original_order_ref
fields.note: Reference to original order being voided

fields.name: void_reason
fields.note: Mandatory reason: salah_input | customer_batal | item_habis | lainnya

fields.name: pin_verified
fields.note: PIN must be verified before void is processed

fields.name: void_amount
fields.note: Total amount voided (affects expected cash in shift)

business_rules: PIN verification required before any void operation
Cancel before payment: order is cancelled, items returned to available, no cash impact
Void after payment (full): full refund tracked, expected cash reduced in shift
Partial void: specific items voided, difference refunded
Void reason is MANDATORY — kasir must select reason
All voids are logged immutably for audit trail
Voided transactions appear in shift closing summary with separate line
Expected cash formula updated: opening + cash_sales - cash_refunds_from_voids
Owner can view all void history with reasons
acceptance_criteria: Cashier can cancel an unpaid order from order list
Cashier can void a paid transaction (requires PIN)
Void reason selection is mandatory
Voided amounts are reflected in shift expected cash calculation
Close shift summary shows void count and total void amount
Owner can see void history in transaction report

should_have.title: Table Management
should_have.description: Visual overview of all restaurant tables with status (available, occupied, reserved). Orders are assigned to specific tables.

user_story.action: see which tables are available or occupied and assign orders to specific tables
user_story.benefit: manage seating efficiently and link orders to the correct table

acceptance_criteria: Visual grid showing all tables with color-coded status
Waiter can tap a table to create or view its active order
Table status updates automatically when order is created/paid
Owner can configure total number of tables

should_have.title: Transaction History
should_have.description: Complete log of all past transactions with search and filter capabilities.

user_story.action: search and review past transactions by date, amount, or payment method
user_story.benefit: maintain financial accountability and resolve any disputes

should_have.description: Complete sales report log with Shift and PIC information. Advanced filtering by date, ID, PIC cashier, Shift, payment method, and total range. Export to Excel for external reporting.
acceptance_criteria: Transaction table shows: date/time, ID, table, Shift ID, PIC Kasir, subtotal, PPN, total, payment method
Transactions can be filtered by: date range, transaction ID, PIC Kasir, Shift ID, payment method, total range
All filters are combinable and have a reset button
Summary stats (count, total revenue, cash total, digital total) update with active filters
Each transaction shows full detail modal with PPN breakdown and Print Struk button
Filtered data can be exported to Excel (.xlsx)
Transaction data cannot be deleted by non-owner users

should_have.title: Low Stock Notification
should_have.description: Automatic alerts when inventory items fall below a defined minimum threshold.

user_story.action: receive automatic notifications when ingredients are running low
user_story.benefit: proactively restock before running out and avoid menu item unavailability

acceptance_criteria: System shows a warning badge when any item is below threshold
Owner can set minimum threshold per inventory item
Low stock items are highlighted in the inventory view
Dashboard shows count of low-stock items

should_have.title: Discount / Promo Management
should_have.description: Apply discounts to orders — percentage or fixed amount. Owner can create promo rules. Discount is visible on payment screen and receipt.

user_story.action: set up discounts and promos that cashiers can apply to orders
user_story.benefit: attract customers and run promotions while tracking their impact on revenue

fields.name: active
fields.note: Boolean — is this discount currently usable

business_rules: Owner creates and manages discount/promo options in settings
Active promos are displayed as banner on order creation screen (S-003) so customer and cashier can see available promos immediately
Cashier can apply discount from order creation screen OR from payment screen
Discount applied BEFORE PPN calculation: Grand Total = (Subtotal - Discount) + PPN
Only one discount per order (prevent stacking)
If promo applied at order creation, it carries through to payment screen
Discount amount and name shown on payment screen and printed receipt
Discounted transactions tracked in sales report
Owner can activate/deactivate discounts anytime
acceptance_criteria: Owner can create/edit/deactivate discount rules in settings
Active promos shown as banner cards on order creation screen (S-003)
Cashier can tap promo banner to apply discount during order creation
Applied promo carries through to payment screen automatically
Cashier can also add/change discount from payment screen
Discount picker shows all active discounts with calculated amount
Payment shows: Subtotal → Diskon → Subtotal setelah diskon → PPN → Grand Total
Receipt includes discount line
Transaction history shows discount info

should_have.title: Report Export to Excel
should_have.description: Export transaction/sales data from Riwayat Transaksi to Excel (.xlsx) for offline accounting and reporting. Export respects active filters.
acceptance_criteria: Export button on Transaction History (S-012) generates Excel file
Export includes all visible columns: date, ID, table, shift, PIC, subtotal, PPN, total, method
Export respects currently active filters (only exports filtered results)
Excel file includes header row and formatted data
Loading indicator shown during export generation

could_have.title: Menu Categorization
could_have.description: Organize menu items into categories for easier browsing during order creation.
acceptance_criteria: Menu items can be assigned to categories
Order screen allows filtering by category
Owner can create, edit, and delete categories

wont_have.title: Online Customer Ordering
wont_have.reason: Out of scope for MVP — focus is on in-restaurant POS operations first

wont_have.title: Multi-branch Support
wont_have.reason: Out of scope for MVP — system is designed for a single restaurant location
